title: Specify slice model.yaml
description: Validates a slice's single typed machine-readable model. This one schema validates both the agent's synthesis-response `model` and the persisted `model.yaml`: kernel-owned fields (`requirements[].id`, `.status`, `requirements[].sources`, `claims[].winner`) and the header fields (`version`, `slice`, `project`) are optional so the agent may omit them and the kernel re-derives/stamps them on projection (normalize, never reject — there is no separate draft schema). Provenance is carried inline (`requirements[].claims[]` with `winner` markers); there is no separate `provenance.yaml` file — `specrun slice provenance` projects the audit view (including `resolution` / `resolution-trace`) from this model plus on-disk Evidence on demand. The model carries only the earned core today — `requirements[]` (load-bearing provenance) and `tasks[]` (requirement→task tracing); the non-requirements sections (`domain` / `apis` / `configuration` / `technical-logic` / `observability`) and their id grammars are deferred until an `execution: tool` target or a contract gate names a sub-tree it consumes. The top-level shape stays closed so a sub-tree can be re-added cleanly when a consumer lands. Operators edit behavioral prose in `spec.md` / `design.md` / `tasks.md`; `model.yaml` is the machine view target builders consume. `target` is not persisted — it is resolved on demand from the slice's bound `project` (the plan stores only `project`); `slice-model-target-drift` checks `model.yaml.project` against `plan.yaml`. Kernel-rendered provenance lines in `spec.md` must match projected ids/status/source lists or `slice-spec-provenance-stale` fires. Closed top-level shape — unknown fields are rejected.

claimId.description: Dotted Evidence claim id (e.g. `password-reset.expiry`); mirrors the id grammar in `evidence.schema.json`.

claimKind.description: Closed claim-kind enum mirrored byte-identically from `evidence.schema.json#/$defs/claimKind`. Carried on each `model.yaml` claim (D13) so the kernel resolves per-kind authority (§Authority over mixed-kind claims) without re-reading Evidence.

agreementVerdict.description: Agent-authored agreement verdict over the contributing claims. Required when more than one claim contributes; omitted for 0 or 1 claim. The kernel applies authority to it to derive `status` and winner markers but never re-decides whether two claim values mean the same thing.

id.description: Kernel-projected in declaration order. The agent omits it in the synthesis response; if present it is ignored and re-derived (normalize, never reject).

status.description: Kernel-projected from the claim count, the agent's `agreement` verdict, and resolved authority. The agent omits it; if present it is ignored and re-derived.

claims.description: Agent-authored claim-level provenance, carried inline (this replaces the former separate `provenance.yaml` `contributing-claims[]`): every `(source, id, kind)` claim that fed this requirement, winners and losers alike. Empty for an `unknown` requirement. `kind` (D13) lets the kernel resolve per-kind authority; the `winner` marker on each entry is kernel-projected. The claim `value` / `path` payload is read from on-disk Evidence by the provenance projection rather than copied here.

sources.description: Kernel-projected rendered source list (highest authority first), mirroring the `Sources:` line rendered into `spec.md`. The agent omits it.

kind.description: Agent-authored: the kind of the cited Evidence claim (D13). The kernel uses it to apply per-kind authority (§Authority over mixed-kind claims) without re-reading Evidence. `slice-model-claim-kind-mismatch` rejects a value that disagrees with the kind recorded for that `(source, id)` in Evidence.

winner.description: Kernel-projected. Present (`true` on the authority-resolved winner, `false` on every dropped loser) only for a `divergence` requirement; absent for `agreed` and `conflict` requirements.
